briefs.title: BC creates new power supply rules for AI data centres and hydrogen production facilities
briefs.summary: BC's new Data Centre Facility and Hydrogen Production Facility Power Supply Regulation sets out how AI data centres, conventional data centres, and hydrogen-for-export facilities can apply for electricity connections through BC Hydro. The rules define what qualifies as an AI data centre (at least 10% of power used for AI tasks), exclude cryptocurrency mining, and establish a formal process — including design deposits (upfront payments to cover the cost of planning grid upgrades) — for getting connected to the provincial power grid.
briefs.action_required: If you operate or plan to open an AI data centre or hydrogen facility in BC, review the new connection process and deposit requirements.

key_dates.label: BC Data Centre & Hydrogen Facility Power Supply Regulation takes effect
